steps.title: Rearrange to standard form
steps.content: Move all terms to one side of the equation. Starting with $x^2 + 13x = -9$, add 9 to both sides: $$x^2 + 13x + 9 = 0$$ Now we can identify the coefficients: $a = 1$, $b = 13$, $c = 9$.

steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content: Use the quadratic formula to solve for $x$. First, calculate the discriminant: $$b^2 - 4ac = 13^2 - 4(1)(9) = 169 - 36 = 133$$ Now substitute all values: $$x = \frac{-13 \pm \sqrt{133}}{2(1)} = \frac{-13 \pm \sqrt{133}}{2}$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
